viralGrowthEngine.description: Proprietary system for identifying and designing viral growth mechanisms
viralGrowthEngine.methodology: Combining network effects + psychological triggers + incentive design

variables.i: Invitation rate (% of users who invite)
variables.cv: Conversion value multiplier (1.0 = same value, 1.5 = 50% more value)

interpretation.kEqualsPointFive: Linear growth (requires paid marketing to sustain)

methods: Embedded invitations (invite during core product use)
Value transfer (new user gets immediate value)
Scarcity (limited beta slots for new users)
Social proof (shows if friends already use it)
Trusted source (invitation from trusted friend > cold)
Network effects (product better with more users)
Exclusive features (early invites get exclusive access)
Timing (invite at moment of highest perceived value)

viralLoopArchetypes.name: Embeds & Network Effect Loop
viralLoopArchetypes.description: Product creates value by being shared/embedded in others' products
examples: Spotify embeds (share song → plays on recipient device)

viralMechanic.step3: Recipient sees artifact in product context
viralMechanic.step4: Recipient needs product to create own artifact
viralMechanic.step5: Recipient joins → creates artifact → shares → repeat

designPrinciples: Make sharing natural (embed, not extra step)
Create value through sharing (recipient sees something)
Lower friction for invited user (instant benefit)
Network effects (product better with more people)

viralLoopArchetypes.name: Exclusive Access Loop
viralLoopArchetypes.description: Early users get exclusive features/access; they invite friends to share benefit

viralMechanic.step1: User gets exclusive access (beta, limited supply)
viralMechanic.step3: User has limited invites to share
viralMechanic.step5: Invited contacts want access (FOMO) → join → invite others

viralLoopArchetypes.name: Reward Referral Loop
viralLoopArchetypes.description: Both referrer and referee get rewarded for successful invitations

viralMechanic.step1: User wants reward (free month, credit, discount)
viralMechanic.step2: User sees referral program (earn reward by inviting)
viralMechanic.step3: User invites contacts with unique link
viralMechanic.step5: Referee appreciates reward → joins → becomes referrer

designPrinciples: Frictionless mechanics (easy to understand, easy to execute)
Progressive rewards (bigger rewards for more referrals)

viralLoopArchetypes.name: Competitive Leaderboard Loop
viralLoopArchetypes.description: Users compete for position; social proof drives signups

viralMechanic.step1: User sees leaderboard of top users/teams
viralMechanic.step2: Competitive drive triggers (want to be on leaderboard)
viralMechanic.step3: User invites team members to beat other teams
viralMechanic.step5: New users see they're behind → join to catch up

designPrinciples: Team participation (need to invite others to compete)

viralLoopArchetypes.name: Content Seeding Loop
viralLoopArchetypes.description: User generates content; content shared goes viral; sharers sign up

viralMechanic.step4: Recipient impressed by quality → shares with own network
viralMechanic.step5: New recipients discover platform through viral content
viralMechanic.step6: Some new recipients join platform to create own content
viralMechanic.step7: New creators → create → share → viral → new creators

designPrinciples: Platform brand in shared links (recognizable, tempting)

viralLoopArchetypes.name: Social Proof + FOMO Loop
viralLoopArchetypes.description: Seeing others use product creates desire; combined with scarcity

viralMechanic.step1: User sees social proof ('50k+ companies use this')
viralMechanic.step4: User feels FOMO (don't want to miss out)
viralMechanic.step6: User becomes social proof for others → repeat

viralLoopArchetypes.kFactorPotential: k = 0.8-2.0 (very high if FOMO is strong)
designPrinciples: Easy trial (remove friction for curious prospects)

viralDesignCanvas.description: Template for designing your viral mechanism

fields.productType: What is your product? (SaaS, Marketplace, Content, Community)
fields.coreUserAction: What do users do in your product? (Create, Share, Consume, Transact)
fields.naturalSharing: Do users naturally want to share it? (YES = viral potential, NO = need incentive)
fields.targetLoop: Which viral loop archetype fits best? (1-6 above)
fields.invitationMechanic: How do users invite? (Link, email, social button, etc.)

incentivesDesign.referrerIncentive: What does inviter get? (Money, access, status, etc.)
incentivesDesign.refereeIncentive: What does invited get? (Same as referrer?)

fields.conversionRate: % of invitations that lead to signup? (Estimate)
fields.conversionValueMultiplier: Do new users become as valuable as referrers? (1.0 = yes)
fields.kFactorInterpretation: Will it go viral exponentially, linear, or need paid?

phase2.name: MVP Launch (Weeks 3-4)
goals: Get user feedback on sharing friction

phase4.name: Scale (Weeks 12+)
goals: Scale paid acquisition (use viral as multiplier)
Optimize referral payouts (balance CAC vs LTV)
Measure network effects (does product improve with scale?)

commonMistakes.consequence: Invitation rate drops 70% if requires >2 clicks
commonMistakes.solution: One-click referral links, pre-filled templates, social sharing buttons

commonMistakes.consequence: Only 2-5% of users ever refer if reward < $20
commonMistakes.solution: Make incentive valuable and immediate (not future, not abstract)

commonMistakes.consequence: Referred users less likely to engage (no incentive)

commonMistakes.solution: Make sharing feel natural, not transactional

metric1.name: K-Factor (Viral Coefficient)
metric1.formula: Invitations sent × Invitation conversion rate × Converted user quality

metric2.name: Invitation Rate
metric2.formula: Users who sent invitations / Total users

metric3.name: Referral Conversion Rate
metric3.formula: Referred users who sign up / Total invitations sent

metric4.name: Viral Doubling Time
metric4.formula: Days to 2x user base through viral alone
